_meta.title: The Printer Protocol Orchard — cross-vendor comparison matrix (machine-readable)

derived_from.source_note: A structured mirror of COMPARISON.md. That prose matrix is a distilled cross-index of the per-family papers; when a cell and a paper disagree, THE PAPER WINS. Regenerate this file from COMPARISON.md, not the reverse.

confidence_legend.source-read: 🟡 read from the vendor's own published slicer/SDK; wire-shape correct, not yet HW-confirmed

_meta.note: `—` means the paper documents no such facet (not 'unknown'). Per-family confidence is a one-glyph summary; individual facts inside each paper carry their own tags, which are authoritative.

families.name: Anycubic
families.auth: slicer mTLS client cert + MQTT creds derived from /info
families.model: poll-hybrid (idle pushes nothing ~48 s; active auto-pushes)

families.name: Bambu
families.auth: user bblp, password = access code off the printer screen
families.model: push (pushall snapshot → live deltas); no command ack

families.name: Creality (stock OS)
families.discovery: manual IP; vendor UDP broadcast; GET /info
families.model: push-hybrid (unsolicited state merged; get poll backstop)
families.progress: % — treat as file-byte (Klipper under the hood)

families.name: Elegoo
families.model: subscribe / passive push + poll floor / push + 1002 poll
families.multi_material: CANVAS 4-slot (material/color/temp; no qty, no RFID UID)

families.name: Klipper / Moonraker
families.discovery: manual IP baseline; optional mDNS _moonraker._tcp / SSDP
families.auth: often none (trusted_clients); else API key (X-Api-Key) / JWT
families.model: subscribe (WS): full snapshot → sparse deltas; or one-shot query
families.multi_material: none native — a software provider (Happy Hare / AFC) layers it

families.name: Duet3D / RRF
families.transport: HTTP :80 — standalone rr_* or SBC/DSF /machine/* REST + 1 WS
families.model: standalone poll-only (no push); SBC push (full-model → patch)
families.multi_material: none (no MMU model) — toolchanger tools[] array

families.name: OctoPrint
families.auth: one API key (X-Api-Key / Bearer); Application-Keys handshake
families.multi_material: none in core — exposes multi-tool; feeders are plugins

families.name: PrusaLink
families.discovery: manual IP; mDNS advertised (service type unconfirmed); GET /api/v1/info
families.auth: HTTP Digest (user maker); legacy X-Api-Key tier (off by default)

families.name: FlashForge
families.transport: newer: pub/sub SDK (closed DLL, MQTT-shaped) / legacy: raw-TCP G-code :8899
families.discovery: newer: UDP broadcast (per-device port); legacy: manual IP → :8899
families.auth: newer: per-device checkCode (screen); legacy: ~M601 handshake, no code
families.progress: see paper (Klipper inside newer; SD-byte on legacy)

families.name: Marlin (USB serial)
families.transport: USB-CDC virtual serial (COMx / ttyUSB* / ttyACM*); baud autodetect
families.discovery: none (OS port enum + M115 probe); VID/PID = UART chip, not printer
families.auth: none — physical access is the authorization
families.progress: synthesized from M27 SD byte position (host-stream: line count)
families.multi_material: none queryable (T<n> lives in the sliced file)

fingerprint.likely: generic Moonraker / Klipper (or a vendor fork)

fingerprint.observe: A Moonraker host that ALSO answers :8100/api
fingerprint.likely: Snapmaker U1 (Moonraker behind a pairing wrapper)

fingerprint.observe: MQTT on :8883 wanting user bblp + an access code; SSDP on :2021
MQTT on :9883 needing a slicer mTLS cert; GET :18910/info returns identity
A plain ws://:9999 carrying flat {method,params}, no auth; :80/upload
JSON-over-WebSocket on :3030 with topic strings; UDP discovery on :3000
A printer running its own MQTT broker on :1883 + :80 upload + access code
GET /rr_model (or /rr_connect) returns JSON; X-Session-Key after login
A /machine/* REST API + one WebSocket at /machine
/api/* with an X-Api-Key header + a SockJS /sockjs push channel
GET /api/v1/status over HTTP Digest (user maker); no push channel
A device answering ASCII G-code on raw TCP :8899 (or UDP SDK discovery + checkCode)
A USB serial port that emits a Marlin start banner and answers M115
